chunks.text: Rikky Muller is an Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, holding the S. Shankar Sastry Professorship in Emerging Technologies. She earned her BS and MEng from MIT and PhD from UC Berkeley, all in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. Currently, she serves as Co-director of the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC), Core Member of the Center for Neural Engineering and Prostheses (CNEP), and Investigator at the Chan-Zuckerberg Biohub.

Dr. Muller previously worked as an IC designer at Analog Devices and co-founded Cortera Neurotechnologies, Inc., a medical device company focused on closed-loop deep brain stimulation technology. Her research group specializes in emerging implantable and wearable medical devices, developing low-power, wireless microelectronic and integrated systems for neurological applications.

She has received numerous honors including being named one of MIT Tech Review's 35 global innovators under 35 (TR35) and Boston MedTech's 40 healthcare innovators under 40. Awards include the National Academy of Engineering Gilbreth Lectureship, NSF CAREER Award, Keysight Early Career Professorship, McKnight Technological Innovations in Neuroscience Award, and IEEE Solid-State Circuits Society New Frontier Award.

Dr. Muller is a Bakar Fellow, Hellman Fellow, and Distinguished Lecturer for the Solid-State Circuits Society. Her education includes a PhD from UC Berkeley (2013), and SB and MEng from MIT (2004). Research areas cover Integrated Circuits, Biosystems & Computational Biology, Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems, and Physical Electronics.

She is affiliated with research centers including the Berkeley Wireless Research Center (BWRC) and Center for Neural Engineering & Prostheses (CNEP). Teaching schedule includes EE 140 Linear Integrated Circuits and EE 240A Analog Integrated Circuits, held on Mondays and Wednesdays from 2:00-3:29 PM in Cory 540AB.

Selected honors and awards from 2023 to 2015 include the Bakar Prize (2023), SSCS New Frontier Award (2021), McKnight Technological Innovations in Neuroscience Award (2020), Bakar Fellows Spark Award (2020), N² Women: Rising Stars (2020), Hellman Fellow (2019), NSF CAREER Award (2019), SSCS Distinguished Lecturer (2019), NAE Gilbreth Lectureship (2017), Keysight Early Career Professor Award (2017), MedTech Boston's 40 Under 40 (2016), and MIT Tech Review TR35 (2015).
